dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7221 IH: Stopping Wall Street From Competing With Main Street Homebuyers Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Luna introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

form.official-title: To amend the Investment Company Act of 1940 to prohibit certain large private funds and registered investment companies from purchasing single family homes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stopping Wall Street From Competing With Main Street Homebuyers Act.

section.header: Restrictions on purchasing single family homes
section.text: The Investment Company Act of 1940 (15 U.S.C. 80a–1 et seq.) is amended by inserting after section 12 the following:

section.header: Restrictions on purchasing single family homes

subsection.text: Beginning on the date that is 90 days after the date of the enactment of this section, a covered fund may not purchase a single family home.

paragraph.text: Not later than 10 years after the date of the enactment of this section, a covered fund may not hold (including through a subsidiary or parent company) any single family homes.

In the case of a covered fund that holds single family homes on the date of the enactment of this section, such fund shall, annually for the 10-year period following such date of enactment, divest of at least 10 percent of such single family homes.
The term covered fund means a registered investment company, real estate investment trust, or private fund—

subparagraph.text: with more than $500,000,000 in assets under management; or
that, during the 1-year period before the date of the enactment of this section—

clause.text: owned 100 or more single-family homes in the United States; or
purchased more than 5 single-family homes in the United States in a 30-day period.

paragraph.text: The term private fund means an issuer that would be an investment company, but for paragraph (1) or (7) of section 3(c).
The term single family home means a residential structure or mobile home which contains one family housing unit.
